title: Joyful Mysteries
mysteries: First Mystery: The Angel Gabriel announces to Mary that she will be the Mother of God.
Second Mystery: Holy Mary goes to visit Elizabeth.
Third Mystery: Jesus is born in Bethlehem.
Fourth Mystery: Jesus is presented in the Temple.
Fifth Mystery: Mary finds Jesus in the Temple.
virtues: Virtue: Let us ask God to grant us humility.
Virtue: Let us ask God to grant us love of neighbor.
Virtue: Let us ask God to grant us a spirit of poverty.
Virtue: Let us ask God to grant us purity of heart.
Virtue: Let us ask God to grant us obedience to our elders.

title: Sorrowful Mysteries
mysteries: First Mystery: Jesus sweats blood for our sake.
Second Mystery: Jesus is scourged for our sake.
Third Mystery: Jesus is crowned with thorns for our sake.
Fourth Mystery: Jesus carries the Cross for our sake.
Fifth Mystery: Jesus dies on the Cross.
virtues: Virtue: Let us ask God to grant us sorrow for sin.
Virtue: Let us ask God to grant us freedom from sins of impurity.
Virtue: Let us ask God to grant us victory over pride.
Virtue: Let us ask God to grant us endurance in hardship.
Virtue: Let us ask God to grant us love for Jesus and Mary.

title: Glorious Mysteries
mysteries: First Mystery: Jesus rises from the dead.
Second Mystery: Jesus ascends into Heaven.
Third Mystery: The Holy Spirit descends upon the Apostles.
Fourth Mystery: The Blessed Virgin Mary is assumed into Heaven.
Fifth Mystery: The Blessed Virgin Mary is crowned Queen in Heaven.
virtues: Virtue: Let us ask God to grant us conversion into holiness.
Virtue: Let us ask God to grant us entry into Heaven.
Virtue: Let us ask God to grant us zeal in our faith.
Virtue: Let us ask God to grant us a good death.
Virtue: Let us ask God to grant us perseverance in the good way.

title: Luminous Mysteries
mysteries: First Mystery: Jesus is baptized in the River Jordan.
Second Mystery: Jesus changes water into wine at Cana.
Third Mystery: Jesus proclaims the Kingdom of God.
Fourth Mystery: Jesus is transfigured.
Fifth Mystery: Jesus institutes the Sacrament of the Eucharist.
virtues: Virtue: Let us ask God to grant us faithfulness to our baptismal promises.
Virtue: Let us ask God to grant us grace to season the world with the leaven of the Gospel.
Virtue: Let us ask God to grant us reception of his Kingdom through true repentance.
Virtue: Let us ask God to grant us grace to brighten the world with the face of Jesus.
Virtue: Let us ask God to grant us grace to sacrifice ourselves for others.
